title: Best Poison Champions for Clan Boss in Raid Shadow Legends
excerpt_html: Looking for the best Poison champions for Clan Boss in Raid Shadow Legends? Discover the top champions that deal consistent boss damage.
content_html:
Best Poison Champions for Clan Boss in Raid Shadow Legends

Poison remains one of the most reliable ways to deal damage in Clan Boss. It does not rely on strong hits or high-end gear, and it scales well as fights go longer. Each stack adds steady damage every time the boss takes a turn, which makes it a core strategy for many teams across all stages of the game.

The strength of Poison teams comes from consistency. With the right champions, you can keep the debuff bar filled and deal damage without needing perfect builds. This makes Poison setups strong for early progression, but they still hold value in late-game Clan Boss teams.

In this guide, we break down the best poison champions for Clan Boss in RAID: Shadow Legends. You will learn which champions stand out, how they work, and how to build a team that keeps damage high from start to finish.

Why Poison is Strong Against Clan Boss

How Poison Damage Works

Poison deals damage when the Clan Boss takes its turn, not when your champion attacks. Each Poison debuff ticks for a set amount of the boss’s MAX HP, which makes it scale well even in longer fights. This means your damage continues to build as long as the debuffs stay active, even if your team is not hitting hard.

Why Poison Is Reliable Damage

Poison remains one of the most consistent damage sources in Clan Boss fights. It does not rely on high stats or strong hits, so even early-game teams can deal strong damage. Once you fill the debuff bar with Poison, the boss will take steady damage every turn. This makes Poison teams easy to build and very effective across all stages of progression.

What Makes a Good Poison Champion

Consistent Poison Placement

Champions who apply Poison often bring far more value in Clan Boss fights. The boss can only hold a limited number of debuffs, so steady application helps keep those slots filled at all times. If Poisons drop off, your damage drops with them. Champions who place poisons every turn, or cycle back to their skills quickly, keep your damage stable across the entire fight.

Poison Sensitivity Champions

Poison Sensitivity increases the damage dealt by all Poison debuffs on the target. This means every Poison tick hits harder, which raises your total damage without needing extra debuffs. When paired with strong poisoners, this effect boosts your team’s output in a simple and reliable way.

Best Poison Champions for Clan Boss

Here we’ve listed 10 of the most important Clan Boss Poisoners in the game: note that this list is not exhaustive, but it should steer you in the right direction for knowing what makes these types of champions so powerful! Check out our Champion Tier List for more info.

OUTLAW MONK

Outlaw Monk, despite his Uncommon rarity, offers one of the fastest early poison setups for Clan Boss. His A2 places two Poison debuffs, which gives new accounts a quick way to start stacking damage. His A1 has a chance to grant an extra turn, and this helps him cycle back to his A2 faster. This loop lets him maintain steady Poison uptime with very little setup, which makes him a strong early-game option.

View Outlaw Monk Guide

FROZEN BANSHEE

Frozen Banshee remains one of the best Rare poisoners for Clan Boss. She places Poison Sensitivity, which boosts all poison damage, then follows up with multiple Poison debuffs through her attacks. Once she locks onto a single target, her output ramps up quickly and stays consistent. This makes her a core pick for many early and mid-game Clan Boss teams.

View Frozen Banshee Guide

Petrifya Rockroot

Petrifya Rockroot brings strong poison output with added utility. She applies Poison debuffs alongside Poison Sensitivity, which raises the damage ceiling for the whole team. She also places Decrease ATK and Decrease DEF, which helps both survival and damage. This mix of debuffs makes her more than just a poisoner.

View Petrifya Rockroot Guide

Kael

Kael offers a simple and reliable poison kit for Clan Boss. His A3 places strong Poison debuffs, which deal high damage over time. His A1 adds smaller poisons, which help fill the debuff bar. This steady flow of poisons makes him a solid starter option that can carry early progression.

View Kael Guide

Dark Kael

Dark Kael brings strong poison synergy with added control tools. His A3 places both Poison debuffs and Poison Sensitivity, which boosts overall damage output. His A1 activates existing poisons, allowing your team to deal burst damage during key turns. He also applies Decrease ATK and extends debuffs, which helps keep poisons active longer. His ability to reduce the boss’s critical rate to 0% with his passive removes spike damage, making him valuable for both damage and survival.

View Dark Kael Guide

Toragi the Frog

Toragi the Frog blends poison output with strong team protection. He passively places Poison debuffs while providing Ally Protect and shields. He also brings Decrease ATK, which reduces incoming damage. This makes him a strong pick for longer Clan Boss runs where survival matters.

View Toragi the Frog Guide

Occult Brawler

Occult Brawler focuses on raw poison output with very little setup. His passive places Poison debuffs each turn, which builds up damage quickly. If you disable his A2, he will use his A1 exclusively, which will add to the amount of Poison debuffs he is putting out. This simple playstyle makes him one of the most consistent poisoners in the game.

View Occult Brawler Guide

Dracomorph

Dracomorph brings top-tier poison damage with strong debuff support. He places Decrease DEF and Weaken, which boosts all damage dealt to the boss. He follows this with heavy Poison application, and his A1 hits hard on its own. This combination makes him one of the strongest Clan Boss champions available if you can keep him alive.

View Dracomorph Guide

NARMA THE RETURNED

Narma the Returned offers one of the most complete poison kits in the game. Her passive places Poison debuffs and reduces the boss’s damage by 25% once five poisons are active. Her A3 applies Poison Sensitivity and up to six poisons, which sets up massive damage. Her A2 adds Decrease ATK, Leech, and activates poisons, then heals allies. Her A1 extends debuffs, which keeps everything active for longer fights.

View Narma the Returned Guide

Xenomorph

Xenomorph stands out as a strong poison-focused Legendary that many players own. His A1 places up to three Poison debuffs on a critical hit, which gives him steady output. His passive adds more poisons when he takes damage, which helps fill the debuff bar. His A3 scales damage based on the number of poisons on the boss, and his passive reduces enemy DEF by 20% when his poisons are active. If you disable his A2, he cycles faster between his A1 and A3, which boosts both damage and poison uptime.

View Xenomorph Guide

Champions That Support Poison Strategies

Debuff Extension Champions

Champions who extend debuffs help keep Poison active (as well as your other important debuffs) for longer without needing to reapply them. This reduces downtime and keeps damage ticking every boss turn. It also frees up turns for other skills, since your poisoners do not need to constantly refresh debuffs. In longer fights, this leads to higher total damage with less effort.

Speed and Turn Meter Champions

Faster teams apply Poison more often and keep debuffs active with less drop-off. Higher Speed means your champions take more turns, so they can place and refresh Poison before it expires. Turn Meter boosts push your team ahead of the boss, which helps maintain control and steady damage. This creates a smooth rotation where Poison stay active across the entire fight.

How to Build Poison Champions for Clan Boss

ACC Requirements

For Clan Boss, your Poison champions need enough ACC to land their debuffs consistently. In this fight, the maximum ACC target you need is 250. Once you reach that point, adding more ACC will not give you extra value against Demon Lord, so you can focus the rest of your build on speed, survival, or damage.

SPD Requirements

The more turns your champion takes, the more often they can place Poison. Faster champions keep the debuff bar filled and raise your total damage over the course of the run.

For general Clan Boss teams, high SPD helps your poisoners cycle back to their skills faster. For speed-tuned teams, each champion must match a set speed range so the rotation stays in sync. In those teams, do not push SPD past the tune.

Recommended Artifact Sets

Sets that give ACC or SPD work best for Clan Boss poisoners. Speed, Perception, and Feral are all strong choices that help you land debuffs and take more turns.

Reflex is another great option, since it can reduce cooldowns and let your champion place Poison more often. Regeneration can be strong on champions who need extra sustain to stay alive deep into the fight.

Blessings and Relics

Lethal Dose does not help in Clan Boss, since it only works in PvP. For PvE boss damage, Brimstone is a strong choice on poison champions that already build ACC.

Arachnelixir is the best relic for a poison-focused build. Golden Elixir is a solid Epic option, and Hoarder’s Cauldron is a good Rare choice that gives extra ACC.

Tips for Maximizing Poison Damage

Keep Debuffs Active

Poison damage only works if the debuffs stay on the boss. If stacks fall off, your damage drops straight away. Keeping the debuff bar filled with Poison at all times gives you steady damage every turn. Champions who apply or refresh Poison often help avoid gaps and keep your total damage high.

Combine Poison With Support Champions

Poison champions perform best when paired with strong support. Clan Boss fights last many turns, so your team needs healing, protection, and damage reduction to survive. Champions with Decrease ATK, Ally Protect, shields, or healing allow your poisoners to stay alive long enough to stack damage. This balance between damage and survival is key for higher Clan Boss scores.

title: Best Poison Champions in Raid Shadow Legends (Clan Boss & Dungeons Guide)
excerpt_html: Looking for the best Poison champions in Raid Shadow Legends? Discover the top champions for Clan Boss, Dragon Dungeon, and boss damage.
content_html:
Best Poison Champions in Raid Shadow Legends (Clan Boss & Dungeons Guide)

Looking for the best Poison champions in Raid Shadow Legends? Discover the top champions for Clan Boss, Dragon Dungeon, and boss damage.

How Poison Works in Raid Shadow Legends

What Poison Debuffs Do

There are two versions of the Poison debuff in RAID: Shadow Legends:

  1. The small version which does 2.5% of an enemy’s MAX HP as damage per turn they take.
  2. The big bersion which does 5% of an enemy’s MAX HP as damage per turn they take.

Additionally, there are a few key effects which can further increase your poison damage:

  1. Poison Sensitivity debuff: this increases the damage enemies take from poison by 25% (e.g. from 5% MAX HP damage to 6.25%).
  2. The Lethal Dose blessing: this increases Poison damage in PvP only (Arena and Siege) by 50%/100%/200% (i.e. from 5% to 7.5%/10%/15%).
  3. The Arachnelixir relic: this increases Poison damage by a % of the poisoner’s ATK stat (from 50% up to 150% extra depending on relic rank).

Note that you can stack all of these effects. So, for example: if you had Poison Sensitivty; a 6-star Lethal Dose blessing; a +18 Arachnelixir relic with 5,000 ATK; and you were fighting an enemy with 100,000 HP in Arena; your Poison would go from 5,000 base damage to 46,875!!!

Of course, Lethal Dose only works in PvP and most bosses these days have hidden passives which drastically reduce Poison damage (usually hard-capping it to a certain amount, such as 50,000, or uncapping it but applying a 90% damage reduction). That being said, Poison will still do steady damage to bosses and can potentially do massive damage to regular enemies anywhere in the game.

Why Poison Is Powerful for Boss Fights

From the section above you might feel like Poison is weak for boss fights. HOWEVER, due to bosses having such incredibly high HP pools–and since Poison debuffs scale their damage off a % of the enemy’s HP–they can actually do very significant damage to bosses.

The strongest effects will be against older bosses such as normal difficulty dungeons or the Demon Lord because these bosses do not have passives to protect them from Poison (or have weaker passives that keeps Poison damage reasonable but still useful).

One of the biggest advantages of Poison is that it requires very little gear to do good damage to bosses. A typical poisoner for bosses only needs enough ACC to land the Poison debuffs, plus having their skills booked and masteries unlocked. Compare this to a crit-based damage dealer, who might need all those things, on top of substantial ATK, C.RATE, C.DMG stats; a top-tier Ignore DEF gear set such as Savage; a damage-boosting relic; and a high level blessing.

In short, a new player will not be able to make their crit-damage dealers hit anywhere near as hard as content creators who have been playing for years, but a Poison debuff against a boss is simply a Poison debuff, and even a new player can build those teams!

Why Poison Champions Are Important

Demon Lord Clan Boss Damage Strategy

As the most important boss in the game (it’s the main source of shards and books), Demon Lord is a top early game priority and Poison champions are a fantastic early game strategy.

Since Poison scales based off the enemy’s MAX HP and has low gear requirements, it’s the perfect choice for new players who lack gear when fighting the incredibly high MAX HP Demon Lord!

It’s very challenging to reach 1 key damage in Ultra-nightmare difficulty with Poison, but it can comfortably take you to a 2 key; a perfect early game achievement!

Dungeon Boss Damage

Poison is also fantastic against early game bosses–namely, normal difficulty of Dragon, Ice Golem, and even Fire Knight bosses.

Dragon is the best one here, and Poison remains relevant even into Hard mode! Dragon is a simple boss, with the key goal being to break his shield to prevent his secret breath attack. Poison debuffs can rip through this shield, and are guaranteed to get value since you can’t push his turn meter back.

Ice Golem is a tricky boss to deal with since he counterattacks at specific HP thresholds. Poison damage, however, bypasses these counterattack triggers, enabling you to drastically reduce the incoming damage in this fight. Poison is the easiest way to take down Stage 25 Normal difficulty, but unfortunately the boss is immune to Poison damage on Hard difficulty.

Fire Knight is not typically a good boss for Poison, since you ideally want him to never take a turn. However, if you bring in an champion like Dark Kael who can Activate Poisons, it becomes a great damage dealing strategy.

Best Poison Champions in Raid

Here we’ve listed 10 of the most important Poisoners in the game: note that this list is not exhaustive, but it should steer you in the right direction for knowing what makes these types of champions so powerful! Check out our Champion Tier List for more info.

KAEL

Kael is the best starter champion, bring strong AoE nukes alongside multiple chances to land Poison. These Poisons will add a ton of “free” damage against many early game bosses, such as Dragon or Demon Lord. Aim to get to 85% C.RATE and 200 ACC, then try to get over 170 SPD for a good starting build!

View Kael Guide

FROZEN BANSHEE

Frozen Banshee is the other well-known rare poisoner. For most bosses you will want to avoid using her A2, and simply place her Poison Sensitivity with her A3 and then follow up with A1s for maximum Poison application. She’s often built very tanky to focus on bosses after your other champions have killed the dungeon waves.

View Frozen Banshee Guide

FAYNE

Fayne is a hybrid damage dealer, bringing not only Poisons but also fantastic debuffs in Decrease DEF, Weaken, and Decrease ATK. On top of this she also does significant damage. Get enough SPD and ACC for her to land debuffs, then focus on her damage output.

View Fayne Guide

VENOMAGE

Venomage is the first champion in our list who specialises in AoE Poison application, applying Poison to all enemies at the same time. She has great supplemental debuffs as well, with her Heal Reduction debuff being particularly useful for the Nether Spider and Mimic bosses.

View Venomage Guide

AKEMTUM

Akemtum has an incredibly unique passive ability which duplicates any Poison damage the enemy team takes onto any enemies who have the Hex debuff–a debuff which he conveniently applies with his A2. Akemtum has a totally unique ability to take down waves–and crucially his bonus damage from his passive can trigger effects such as Stuns from the Stun set, opening up some incredible sneaky strategies.

View Akemtum Guide

KALVALAX

Kalvalax has one of the most infamous Poison abilities in the game, putting out four Poison debuffs at the start of every round. This makes him an absolute MVP for any Poison speed-farming teams, allowing you to quickly blanket the enemy in Poisons so that a Poison Activation champion can instantly wipe out an entire wave.

View Kalvalax Guide

BALAR THE LOST

Balar has a very complete kit for a Poisoner, bringing not only AoE Poisons but also Poison Activation, Poison Sensitivity–and even more! He is particularly adept at the Chimera Clan boss, due to his high personal damage potential alongside the sheer amount of buffs and debuffs in his kit, which opens up a huge amount of Chimera Trials.

View Balar the Lost Guide

NARMA THE RETURNED

Narma the Returned was recently reworked, making her another very complete Poisoner. She can pump out AoE Poisons and Poison Sensitivity with her A3, then Activate Poisons with her A2. She also can bring a solid amount of healing and damage reduction to your team, helping them to stay alive. Unfortunately, she’s a notorious bibliophile who  needs a crazy amount of books to fully unlock all her skills.

View Narma the Returned Guide

TEODOR THE SAVANT

Teodor brings in AoE Poisons, Poison and HP Burn activation, plus both Increase and Decrease SPD. The truly special thing, however, is that he increase the duration of Poisons and Burns before activating them, helping him to maintain those debuffs compared to other activators who can quickly rip through them.

View Teodor the Savant Guide

NELL BLACKTEETH

Nell is currently the only mythical champion who focuses on Poison and she is absurdly powerful. Nell’s true power is revealed in PvP, where her passive skill can pump out an overwhelming amount of unblockable Poisons, ripping through any buff-heavy teams. To maximise her power, you will need both the Lethal Dose blessing and the Arachnelixir relic, but the effect is worth it. Note that she is extremely powerful in the new Mercurial gear set but she will also struggle against any enemies who have this set in return.

View Nell Blackteeth Guide

How to Build Poison Champions

ACC Requirements

The most important thing for Poisoners is to have enough ACC to land your Poisons. To land debuffs consistently, you should aim to have about 25 ACC more than the enemy’s RES stat. Use our Raid Stages Tool to check any enemy in any area of the game. Dragon 20, for example, has 200 RES, so you should aim for 225 ACC.

SPD Requirements

The more turns you take, the more Poisons you can apply!

We generally recommend running medium-high SPD on your Poison champions to maximise the amount of Poisons you will have. Note that for speed-tuned Demon Lord teams, you will have to build your champions with very specific speeds (check those guides if you are using such a team).

Recommended Artifact Sets

Any gear sets which give ACC or SPD are fantastic, such as Speed, Perception, or Feral sets.

Special mention can be made of Reflex and Regeneration sets: Reflex reduces your cooldowns, allowing you to palce your Poisons more often; Regeneration gives you some healing every turn, which often plays a key role in solo teams using Poison champions.

Blessings and Relics

The Lethal Dose blessing is a specialised Poison blessing but it only works in PvP (Arena and Siege). For PvE content, consider Brimstone (it synergises well with Poisoners who are often ACC-building, anti-boss specialists).

The Arachnelixir relic is the best specialised Poison relic, and makes ATK a top priority stat. Golden Elixir (Epic) or the Hoarder’s Cauldron (Rare) are both good budget options, giving self-healing and bonus ACC respectively.

LET US KNOW HOW YOU USE POISON CHAMPIONS IN THE COMMENTS!

title: Titus Blackplume – Hydra Clan Boss Guide
excerpt_html: In this guide we break down how to build a Hydra Clan Boss team with Titus Blackplume in RAID: Shadow Legends.
content_html:
Titus Blackplume – Hydra Clan Boss Guide

In this guide we’ll break down everything you need to know about building a Titus Blackplume team for Hydra Clan Boss!

For even more information about Titus, check out:

  1. Our Titus champion page
  2. Our Titus champion spotlight
  3. Our Titus Demon Lord guide
  4. Our Titus Chimera guide

CRAZY DAMAGE POTENTIAL

Titus is currently the second best damage dealer for Hydra in the entire game–second only to Trunda. At maximum potential, Titus can leave even other amazing damage characters like Kro’khad in the dust (with Kro’khad’s record sitting at around 2 billion, Titus can do almost 20 times more damage than him…).

The reason Titus deals so much damage is his A2: the short version is that the bigger your Shields are, the harder he will hit—potentially for MILLIONS of damage with every use of this skill.

For a full breakdown of the mechanics behind this, check out our Titus Champion Spotlight.

For this guide, the key thing to know is that: BIG SHIELDS = BIG DAMAGE!!

NERFS?

Longer-term players will remember the old Corpulent Cadaver teams; these worked in essentially the same way as Titus, but Cadaver was nerfed a couple of years ago due to his performance in Hydra Clash. There was a similar team built around Yannica, and this was also nerfed.

As of the time of writing, Plarium has officially stated that they will not nerf Titus. This means that Titus is currently the only champion with significant damage scaling from Shield buffs, and he should not be changed in future. BUT… The community is very skeptical about Plarium’s “no Titus nerf” stance, so have a back-up plan just in case they change their minds.

SHIELDS ARE THE KEY

There are currently 3 different types of Shields in RAID: Shadow Legends:

  1. Shields from gear sets.
  2. Shields from abilities.
  3. Shields from the Volcardiac Heart Mythical Relic.

Each of these Shields can be extended and grown (see the next sections!) and each will contribute to Titus’ damage.

Ideally, you want each of these Shields present in your Titus team (though good luck getting the Mythical Relic!). It will work with only one type, but bringing a second type of Shield is not hard and will effectively double your damage.

NOTE: the Shield set is the ideal set for Shields from gear sets, since it gives a strong Shield. Bolster is NOT viable, since it cannot be extended, and will therefore disappear after 3 turns no matter what.

SHIELD GROWERS

Now that you have at least 2 types of Shield in your team, the next step is to grow these Shields, since the bigger the Shield, the bigger the damage boost for Titus.

There are very few champions in the game who can do this: for Hydra, you are looking at Vault Keeper Wixwell, Underpriest Brogni, or even Bloodshield accessories.

WIXWELL

Vault Keeper Wixwell if by far the best option for any Titus team, bringing much stronger Shield growth than other options, alongside the Increase Defence buff and the Decrease Attack debuff to drastically mitigate incoming damage against the Shields..

Wixwell’s ability to grow Shields comes from the number of buffs on your team, so look for champions who bring the buffs he’s missing to grow your shields faster.

BROGNI

Underpriest Brogni is also viable, albeit a weaker and more demanding option than Wixwell. Brogni’s ability to grow shields depends on his own damage dealt, so he works well against the Hydra where he has multiple targets and a huge damage boost against decapitated heads. He also brings Block Debuffs to prevent the most debuffs in the fight.

Unlike Wixwell, you will have to find Decrease Attack, Increase Defence, and Buff Extension elsewhere in your team composition.

Brogni’s ability to grow Shields comes from his damage, so build him with 100% C.RATE% and as much ATK and C.DMG% as possible. 6-piece Merciless is ideal for both the Ignore Defence and chance for Cooldown Reduction.

BLOODSHIELD AND OTHER OPTIONS

Bloodshield accessories are an interesting option for Hydra (but not other Clan Bosses). Due to the massively increased damage that decapitated heads take, you can start to snowball a powerful Shield buff once you have your first head down. This will be much weaker than using Brogni or Wixell, but is a viable option for players who lack these champions.

BUFF EXTENDERS

With your Titus and Shield Growth champion locked in, the only other core requirement to make the team function is to bring 3 champions who can Extend Buffs on a 3-turn cooldown.

The goal here simple: we must maintain the same Shield buffs (which we grow from the very start of the fight) on Titus at all times.

The method is also simple: if we bring 3 champions who can extend buffs by 1 turn every 3 turns, then every 3 turns we will have extended our buffs by 3 turns total, and they will never expire!

GREAT BUFF EXTENDER OPTIONS

Wixwell himself can extend buffs on a 3-turn cooldown, so you need two others for his team; Brogni will need three extenders alongside him and Titus.

Krisk the Ageless is a classic example, who also brings Ally Protection, thereby soaking up 50% of the damage your Shields would take.

Mad Hatter was a fusion champion this year, so is a strong option many will have. His Ally Attack is not particularly useful for Titus, but his Weaken debuff is!

Demytha is an Epic option, who will also protect your Shields from some damage with her Block Damage buff.

Lady Mikage is a permanent Mythical fusion, who will supplement your team with Debuff Extension and Increase Crit Damage as well.

REFLEX SETS AND COOLDOWN REDUCTION

If you lack the ideal buff extension setup, you can potentially use a 4-turn buff extender like Lanakis the Chosen by building your extension champions in Reflex, Impulse, or 4-piece Merciless sets. Each of these sets gives you a chance to reduce your cooldowns, which can make up for a suboptimal setup if you run enough of them.

Reflex is always a great option for Wixwell, since it not only enhances his Buff Extension, but also his Shield Growth.

BUILDING THE TEAM

Unlike the other Clan Bosses, you have 6 team members when fighting the Hydra, which opens up a lot of flexibility.

When building a standard Titus team (i.e. one which doesn’t utilise Reset champions–as discussed in the next section), follow these team building guidelines to up your effectiveness.

To help your Shields take less damage, look for champions with:

  1. Decrease Attack
  2. Strengthen
  3. Increase Defence

Next, look for champions with:

  1. Decrease Defence
  2. Weaken
  3. Reduce Skill Cooldown

These will help your Titus (and other champions) hit much harder, or use their key skills more often.

Finally, look for gear sets such as:

  1. 9-piece Protection on buffers, for 5% more damage per buff.
  2. 9-piece Feral on debuffers, for 5% more damage per debuff.

Additional damage boosts will push Titus’damage even further!

DEALING WITH THE HYDRA MECHANICS

Aside from these general team building tips, you will also need to handle the typical Hydra mechanics that the heads will do.

The Head of Mischief is the biggest problem, since he can potentially Steal the Shield buffs off Titus! To deal with him try:

  1. Building Titus with enough RES to resist the buff steals.
  2. Bring another champion with self-buffs and high RES to be the “Mischief tank”.

Use our Raid Stages Tool to figure out how much RES you need for the difficulty you are currently facing!

To deal with Head of Decay you will need a champion with Provoke.

To deal with Head of Torment you will need a champion who can give your team Perfect Veil, or who can regularly Remove Debuffs. You can also just hope for good luck if you’re daring…

To deal with Head of Blight, Suffering and Wrath, bring Block Buffs to prevent run-ending buffs such as Poison Cloud or Reflect Damage.

You should also bring a Hex debuff in your team, since this adds a huge amount of damage due spreading 10% of the big single-target hits you will be dealing against this boss.

RESET CHAMPIONS UNLOCK RECORD DAMAGE

To push your Titus team to the highest potential, you will need to use Cooldown Reduction champions.

The goal is simple: have Wixwell and Titus use their A2 abilities every single turn they take.

Since this removes the requirement to bring other Buff Extenders, it opens up your team building to include damage boosting champions such as Gurptuk.

LAMASU AND YUMEKO

The ultimate team uses Authoratrix Lamasu and Yumeko together: Yumeko resets the entire teams’ cooldowns with her A3, then Lamasu resets Yumeko with her A3.

This team is incredibly powerful, with the major downside being that it will require manual play to ensure Lamasu targets her A3 on Yumeko.

FULL AUTO VARIANTS

An auto-friendly team can be built by stacking several champions who give smaller cooldown reductions to your entire team (i.e. they phsyically cannot target the wrong champion!).

Consider champions such as:

  • Lady of Ireth
  • Emic Trunkheart
  • Fjorad Wolfheart

title: Titus Blackplume – Chimera Clan Boss Guide
excerpt_html: In this guide we break down how to build a Chimera Clan Boss team with Titus Blackplume in RAID: Shadow Legends.
content_html:
Titus Blackplume – Chimera Clan Boss Guide

In this guide we’ll break down everything you need to know about building a Titus Blackplume team for Chimera Clan Boss. Stay tuned for our upcoming guide for Titus in Hydra Clan Boss as well!

For even more information about Titus, check out:

  1. Our Titus champion page
  2. Our Titus champion spotlight
  3. Our Titus Demon Lord Clan Boss guide

CRAZY DAMAGE POTENTIAL

Titus is currently the strongest damage dealer for Chimera Clan Boss in the game! (The non-Titus UNM record is 1.9B with Toshiro; Titus’ record is currently sitting at 8.1B…)

The reason Titus deals so much damage is his A2: the short version is that the bigger your Shields are, the harder he will hit—potentially for MILLIONS of damage with every use of this skill.

For a full breakdown of the mechanics behind this, check out our Titus Champion Spotlight.

For this guide, the key thing to know is that: BIG SHIELDS = BIG DAMAGE!!

NERFS?

Longer-term players will remember the old Corpulent Cadaver teams; these worked in essentially the same way as Titus, but Cadaver was nerfed a couple of years ago due to his performance in Hydra Clash. There was a similar team built around Yannica, and this was also nerfed.

As of the time of writing, Plarium has officially stated that they will not nerf Titus. This means that Titus is currently the only champion with significant damage scaling from Shield buffs, and he should not be changed in future. BUT… The community is very skeptical about Plarium’s “no Titus nerf” stance, so have a back-up plan just in case they change their minds.

THERE IS ONE DOWNSIDE…

Titus teams are the best for damage and the best for Chimera Clash points as well, but there is one thing this team struggles with: Trials.

Trials are essential for maximising the amount of Relic crafting materials you will get on your account, and Titus teams simply can’t do very many of them due to the strict team building requirements for enabling Titus (explained in the next section).

What this means for you is that you want to build two types of Chimera team (if you can!):

  1. A Titus team (from this guide) for whenever you need to do maximum Clash points.
  2. A team dedicated purely to doing Trials (particularly ones that give mythical materials) for weeks when you do not need to score high Clash points for your Clan.

SHIELDS ARE THE KEY

There are currently 3 different types of Shields in RAID: Shadow Legends:

  1. Shields from gear sets.
  2. Shields from abilities.
  3. Shields from the Volcardiac Heart Mythical Relic.

Each of these Shields can be extended and grown (see the next sections!) and each will contribute to Titus’ damage.

Ideally, you want each of these Shields present in your Titus team (though good luck getting the Mythical Relic!). It will work with only one type, but bringing a second type of Shield is not hard and will effectively double your damage.

NOTE: the Shield set is the ideal set for Shields from gear sets, since it gives a strong Shield. Bolster is NOT viable, since it cannot be extended, and will therefore disappear after 3 turns no matter what.

SHIELD GROWTH

Now that you have at least 2 types of Shield in your team, the next step is to grow these Shields, since the bigger the Shield, the bigger the damage boost for Titus.

There are very few champions in the game who can do this: for Chimera, you are looking at Vault Keeper Wixwell as the only realistic option.

WIXWELL

Vault Keeper Wixwell is essential for Chimera, bringing much stronger Shield growth than other options, alongside the Increase Defence buff and the Decrease Attack debuff to drastically mitigate incoming damage against the Shields. His Intercept buffs can also prevent Provokes and other CC debuffs from the Chimera.

Wixwell’s ability to grow Shields comes from the number of buffs on your team, so look for champions who bring the buffs he’s missing to grow your shields faster.

An example Wixwell team is: Vault Keeper Wixwell; Krisk the Ageless; Lydia the Deathsiren; Oella; Titus Blackplume.

BLOODSHIELD, BROGNI, AND OTHER OPTIONS

From our testing, these are viable options for Hydra Clan Boss, and some can work against Demon Lord. Unfortunately, due to the high incoming damage from Chimera from the very start of the fight, they do not currently seem to be viable for this boss; if you don’t have a Wixwell to pair with your Titus, you are better off building a non-Titus team instead.

BUFF EXTENDERS

With your Titus and Wixwell locked in, the next key requirement is to bring 2-3 champions who can Extend Buffs on a 3-turn cooldown.

The goal here simple: we must maintain the same Shield buffs (which we grow from the very start of the fight) on Titus at all times.

Three champions is the simplest method: if we bring 3 champions who can extend buffs by 1 turn every 3 turns, then every 3 turns we will have extended our buffs by 3 turns total, and they will never expire!

COOLDOWN REDUCTION IS KING

It is also viable to bring less than 3 buff extenders (or even just use Wixwell on his own) if you bring reliable sources of cooldown reduction, such as using a Lamasu and Yumeko in manual play to constantly reset Wixwell’s A2.

As you’ll see in the next section, all of the top teams will be using this combo.

Even without the OP Yumeko combo, it is still worth building sets such as Reflex, 4-piece Merciless, Impulse, etc. on your Wixwell so that he can cast his A2 more often; incoming damage in Chimera is high, so the quicker you grow the Shield, the easier it is to get a large one.

GREAT BUFF EXTENDER OPTIONS

Wixwell himself can extend buffs on a 3-turn cooldown, so you need one/two others for his team

Krisk the Ageless is a classic example, who also brings Ally Protection, thereby soaking up 50% of the damage your Shields would take.

Mad Hatter was a fusion champion this year, so is a strong option many will have. His Ally Attack is not particularly useful for Titus, but his Weaken debuff is!

Oella is an old fusion who will make team building much easier thanks to her Increase Resistance buff.

Androc the Glorious is essentially the mythical version of Oella in his base form, bringing even more buffs and turn meter boosts.

Lady Mikage is a permanent Mythical fusion, who will supplement your team with Debuff Extension and Increase Crit Damage as well.

BUILDING THE TEAM

RESISTANCE IS NOT FUTILE

The final step to building the team is to understand the crucial difference between Demon Lord and Chimera: the Chimera will try to remove all your buffs twice per Ultimate Form, and will try to steal your buffs at least once per Viper Form.

Because of this, it is essential to build Titus with enough Resistance to prevent these buff removals/steals from happening. If his Shields ever get removed, you’re back to square one on his damage and likely need to restart the entire run.

Here are the minimum recommended RES goals for each difficulty for your Titus for the Ultimate/Viper forms:

  • Easy 205/255
  • Normal 255/305
  • Hard 305/355
  • Brutal 355/405
  • Nightmare 455/505
  • Ultranightmare 505/555

Note that there will give you around a 90% chance to resist each effect from the Ultimate/Viper forms; it is worthwhile building even higher than this if possible to gain an even better chance to resist.

3% HURTS

Due to how ACC/RES work in RAID: Shadow Legends, the Chimera will always have a 3% chance to successfully remove your buffs regardless of how much RES you build.

Unfortunately, there is nothing you can do to completely prevent this.

One step you can take is to ensure that your Shield buff from abilities is placed by someone geared in a Protection set, so that Shield will be protected.

Another option is to use the Lightning Cage blessing on Titus, though this will not reliably stack and activate when you want it to, and will be a big damage loss as well.

In reality, even after taking these steps, you will simply have to build high RES and restart runs where the 3% happens. Try not to have too much fun(!)

MANUAL IS BEST

As you can see from the example above, the best possible team comp is: Authoratrix Lamasu; Yumeko; Vault Keeper Wixwell; Senna Amberheart; Titus Blackplume.

Lamasu can place Block Buffs and Decrease Speed on the boss as needed; otherwise she resets Yumeko.

Yumeko spam resets your whole team; Lamasu resets her in turn, letting her do this every turn.

Wixwell will place his Shield on the first turn, then spam his A2 thanks to resets.

Titus will spam his A2, except against Ram Form where he will only A1.

Senna will spam her A2 and revive if anyone dies.

NUB’S TEAM (FULL AUTO)

My team is a full auto team: Androc the Glorious; Krisk the Ageless; Lydia the Deathsiren; Vault Keeper Wixwell; Titus Blackplume.

Full auto means much lower damage, but much higher convenience every week!

This team works well, but lacks a reviver which can be dangerous due to the high damage coming at Krisk from Ally Protection. If you lack the gear to keep Krisk alive, swap to a different buff extender instead, such as Mad Hatter.

Androc can be replaced by Oella, but losing his passive RES bonuses will raise the RES stat needed on Titus. He is there for Buff Extension, Strengthen, Increase Resistance and lots of healing. I have him in 9-piece Protection to turn every buff he brings into 5% more damage.

Wixwell is geared in a Shield set, since I only have one good Shield set on my account and he will be in every single Titus team, keeping things simple.

Krisk brings Buff Extension, Decrease Defence, Decrease Attack, and Ally Protection. He’s geared in 9-piece Protection to ensure his buffs are protected.

Lydia is in 9-piece Feral for boosting damage, and her Decrease Defence and Weaken debuffs are essential for Titus to deal damage (people with 6-star Crushing Rend on Titus can skip Decrease Defence, but not otherwise).

Titus is in 6-piece Merciless and 2-piece Zeal. He also has a Cat’s Gaze Relic. Due to Androc, I didn’t need to intentionally build any RES.

HAVE YOU USED TITUS IN CHIMERA? LET US KNOW IN THE COMMENTS!

title: Titus Blackplume – Demon Lord Clan Boss Guide
excerpt_html: In this guide we break down how to build a Demon Lord Clan Boss team with Titus Blackplume in RAID: Shadow Legends.
content_html:
Titus Blackplume – Demon Lord Clan Boss Guide

In this guide we’ll break down everything you need to know about building a Titus Blackplume team for Demon Lord Clan Boss. Stay tuned for our guides for Titus in the Hydra and Chimera Clan Bosses as well.

For even more information about Titus, check out:

  1. Our Titus champion page
  2. Our Titus champion spotlight

CRAZY DAMAGE POTENTIAL

Titus is currently one of the strongest damage dealers for Demon Lord Clan Boss that we have ever seen, capable of literally breaking the boss by exceeding the 2.14 BILLION damage cap (see screenshot above for an example).

The reason Titus deals so much damage is his A2: the short version is that the bigger your Shields are, the harder he will hit—potentially for MILLIONS of damage with every use of this skill.

For a full breakdown of the mechanics behind this, check out our Titus Champion Spotlight.

For this guide, the key thing to know is that: BIG SHIELDS = BIG DAMAGE!!

NERFS?

Longer-term players will remember the old Corpulent Cadaver teams; these worked in essentially the same way as Titus, but Cadaver was nerfed a couple of years ago due to his performance in Hydra Clash. There was a similar team built around Yannica, and this was also nerfed.

As of the time of writing, Plarium has officially stated that they will not nerf Titus. This means that Titus is currently the only champion with significant damage scaling from Shield buffs, and he should not be changed in future. BUT… The community is very skeptical about Plarium’s “no Titus nerf” stance, so have a back-up plan just in case they change their minds.

SHIELDS ARE THE KEY

There are currently 3 different types of Shields in RAID: Shadow Legends:

  1. Shields from gear sets.
  2. Shields from abilities.
  3. Shields from the Volcardiac Heart Mythical Relic.

Each of these Shields can be extended and grown (see the next sections!) and each will contribute to Titus’ damage.

Ideally, you want each of these Shields present in your Titus team (though good luck getting the Mythical Relic!). It will work with only one type, but bringing a second type of Shield is not hard and will effectively double your damage.

NOTE: the Shield set is the ideal set for Shields from gear sets, since it gives a strong Shield. Bolster is NOT viable, since it cannot be extended, and will therefore disappear after 3 turns no matter what.

SHIELD GROWERS

Now that you have at least 2 types of Shield in your team, the next step is to grow these Shields, since the bigger the Shield, the bigger the damage boost for Titus.

There are very few champions in the game who can do this: for Demon Lord, you are looking at Vault Keeper Wixwell or Underpriest Brogni.

WIXWELL

Vault Keeper Wixwell if by far the best option for Demon Lord, bringing much stronger Shield growth than other options, alongside the Increase Defence buff and the Decrease Attack debuff to drastically mitigate incoming damage against the Shields. His Intercept buffs can also prevent Stuns from the Demon Lord.

The gearing for this team is also incredibly simple, and you will easily hit the one-key for even UNM if you have enough Buff Extenders (discussed in the next section of the guide!).

Wixwell’s ability to grow Shields comes from the number of buffs on your team, so look for champions who bring the buffs he’s missing to grow your shields faster.

An example Wixwell team is: Vault Keeper Wixwell; Lady Mikage; Mad Hatter; Renegade; Titus Blackplume.

BROGNI

Underpriest Brogni is also viable, albeit a weaker and more demanding option than Wixwell. Brogni’s ability to grow shields depends on his own damage dealt, so he will perform much better against the Hydra where he has multiple targets and decapitated head damage boosts. In his favour, he will do more personal damage than Wixwell thanks to his passive damage reflection and Max HP procs, and he also brings Block Debuffs to prevent the Demon Lord placing Stuns or Decrease Speed etc.

Unlike Wixwell, you will have to find Decrease Attack, Increase Defence, and Buff Extension elsewhere in your team composition.

Brogni’s ability to grow Shields comes from his damage, so build him with 100% C.RATE% and as much ATK and C.DMG% as possible. 6-piece Merciless is ideal for both the Ignore Defence and chance for Cooldown Reduction.

An example Brogni team is: Lady Mikage; Underpriest Brogni; Mad Hatter; Krisk the Ageless; Titus Blackplume.

BLOODSHIELD AND OTHER OPTIONS

From our testing, these are viable options for Hydra Clan Boss, but not for Demon Lord. Without Wixwell or Brogni, you are better off using a different Demon Lord team than trying to use Titus.

BUFF EXTENDERS

With your Titus and Shield Growth champion locked in, the only other core requirement to make the team function is to bring 3 champions who can Extend Buffs on a 3-turn cooldown.

The goal here simple: we must maintain the same Shield buffs (which we grow from the very start of the fight) on Titus at all times.

The method is also simple: if we bring 3 champions who can extend buffs by 1 turn every 3 turns, then every 3 turns we will have extended our buffs by 3 turns total, and they will never expire!

GREAT BUFF EXTENDER OPTIONS

Wixwell himself can extend buffs on a 3-turn cooldown, so you need two others for his team; Brogni will need three extenders alongside him and Titus.

Hellborn Sprite is an amazing accessible Rare option, who also brings Weaken, giving you a great debuff as well.

Krisk the Ageless is a classic example, who also brings Ally Protection, thereby soaking up 50% of the damage your Shields would take.

Mad Hatter was a fusion champion this year, so is a strong option many will have. His Ally Attack is not particularly useful for Titus, but his Weaken debuff is!

Demytha is an Epic option, who will also protect your Shields from some damage with her Block Damage buff.

Lady Mikage is a permanent Mythical fusion, who will supplement your team with Debuff Extension and Increase Crit Damage as well.

REFLEX SETS AND COOLDOWN REDUCTION

If you lack the ideal buff extension setup, you can potentially use a 4-turn buff extender like Lanakis the Chosen by building your extension champions in Reflex, Impulse, or 4-piece Merciless sets. Each of these sets gives you a chance to reduce your cooldowns, which can make up for a suboptimal setup if you run enough of them.

Reflex is always a great option for Wixwell, since it not only enhances his Buff Extension, but also his Shield Growth.

BUILDING THE TEAM

With Titus, Wixwell/Brogni, and 3 Buff Extenders, you now have an amazing Demon Lord team.

To push it further, look for champions with:

  1. Decrease Attack
  2. Strengthen
  3. Increase Defence

These will help your Shields take less damage, thereby letting Titus do more damage, faster, for longer.

Next, look for champions with:

  1. Decrease Defence
  2. Weaken
  3. Reduce Skill Cooldown

These will help your Titus (and other champions) hit much harder, or use their key skills more often.

Finally, look for gear sets such as:

  1. 9-piece Protection on buffers, for 5% more damage per buff.
  2. 9-piece Feral on debuffers, for 5% more damage per debuff.

Additional damage boosts will push Titus’damage even further!

TEAM PRESETS

Set up your speeds and presets so that:

  1. You place your Shield buff(s).
  2. You then have your Buff Extenders increase their duration.
  3. Set your Buff Extension and/or Shield Growth abilities to top priority after the opener (if running any cooldown reduction).
  4. Turn off Titus’ A3 entirely, and have him prioritise his A2.

The goal of the setup is simply to make sure that your Shields are being Extended and Grown right from the start. It is essential that Titus does not use his A3, since his Ally Protection buff will cause him to take extra damage to his Shields.

Speeds are very forgiving with this setup, and there are no essential stat goals.

NUB’S TEAM

My team for UNM Demon Lord is: Vault Keeper Wixwell; Krisk the Ageless; Androc the Glorious; Hellborn Sprite; and Titus.

This is one of the strongest possible Titus teams right now, but you can easily replace them with other options, as discussed above!

Wixwell is geared in a Shield set, since I only have one good Shield set on my account and he will be in every single Titus team, keeping things simple.

Krisk brings Buff Extension, Decrease Defence, Decrease Attack, and Ally Protection. I have him in 9-piece Feral right now, though I may switch him to Protection for Chimera (his Shield buff being Protected will crucially stop the Chimera from removing it, limiting run RNG).

Androc is there for Buff Extension, Strengthen, and lots of buffs (these help Wixwell do more Shield Growth). I have him in 9-piece Protection to turn every buff he brings into 5% more damage.

Hellborn Sprite brings Buff Extension and Weaken. They also crucially fulfil the “Beat Demon Lord with a Rare Champion on your Team” advanced quest(!) and they’re in random gear.

Titus is in 6-piece Merciless and 2-piece Zeal. He also has a Cat’s Gaze Relic. As you can see, all of this is unnecessary since we have well exceeded the 2.14B damage cap(!).

title: Lydia the Deathsiren – Champion Spotlight
excerpt_html: In this Spotlight we break down everything you need to know about Lydia the Deathsiren, updated for 2025, in Raid: Shadow Legends.
content_html:
Lydia the Deathsiren – Champion Spotlight

Lydia the Deathsiren has been around for a long time, but, with her 6-star soul up for grabs in a Champion Training Tournament this week, we decided this was the perfect time to do an updated Champion Spotlight for her.

Lydia is the final reward for Faction Wars normal difficulty and has been a staple of the meta since her release!

Check out our dedicated Champion Page for Lydia for even more info.

LYDIA SKILLS EXPLAINED

Rather than break down every ability in detail, as we usually do in these Champion Spotlights, for this one we will instead give an overview of why Lydia is still relevant in 2025 since she’s such a well established champion.

SHE HAS ONE OF THE BEST MOVES IN THE GAME

Lydia’s A2, Siren’s Wail, is one of the most value-packed skills in the entire game. AoE Decrease Defence and Weaken together in one move is a Void Legendary calibre ability on its own–check out champions such as Venus, Belanor, or Yncensa for example. Not only does Lydia bring these debuffs, but she also brings Increase Speed and Strengthen for her whole team on the same move as well–these two buffs together would basically qualify for an entire core skill for a typical legendary. This has classically synergised extremely well with Seer’s A3.

In short, Lydia essentially brings two abilities of value in her A2, which has kept her as the premiere option for many teams.

CHIMERA TRIALS

Chimera Clan Boss released at the end of 2024 and one of the key aspects to success is completing as many Trials as possible. Lydia stands out as one of the best champions in the entire game for completing Trials, bringing not only all the core buffs and debuffs of her A2, but opening up the Ram Form with her A1 Fears and the Viper with her A3 Block Active Skills–both rare debuffs which are hard to find on good champions for this fight.

Lydia enables 12 total Trials–to put this in context, we originally thought about dividing Trial Scores by 2 to give a 5-star rating similarly to how we do other ratings on the site, but Lydia has such a high score that the system would have been broken(!)

HOW TO BUILD LYDIA

Best Stats

The key stats to look for on Lydia are:

  1. SPD
  2. ACC
  3. HP

There are several ways to build Lydia which can drastically change her stat priority:

  • For a typical build, go for lots of SPD alongside enough ACC to place her debuffs and enough HP/DEF to survive.
  • It’s also possible to build RES (a decent option for Clan Bosses or Faction Wars Hard), by sacrificing some SPD or HP/DEF to do so.
  • Another build adds in high ATK, C.RATE% and C.DMG% as well to maximise her personal damage. This is usually for speed farming dungeons, where every bit of damage will make your runs faster.

Best Gear Sets

The 5 best sets for Lydia are:

  1. Protection
  2. Feral
  3. Merciless
  4. Reflex
  5. Perception

9-pieces of Protection is her most common top-end build, since it gives great stats and a 10% damage boost to the team thanks to her 2 buffs. Protecting her buffs is also very useful for situations where enemies can remove them, such as Chimera or Faction Wars Hard.

9-pieces of Feral is a higher damage option but you lose the protected buffs. Lydia can reliably place 4 debuffs on any target (Decrease Defence, Weaken, Block Buffs, Poison Sensitivity), and even more than that situationally (Brimstone, Fear, and Block Active Skills): this gives you between a 20% to 35% damage boost if you can place them succesfully.

Merciless and Reflex are both good options for reducing her cooldowns. 6-or-9-piece Merciless is the top option for a high damage build.

Perception or other similar sets are good accessible options too; anything giving her key stats is valuable.

Best Masteries

Check out our Lydia the Deathsiren Champion Page for multiple options for masteries!

BEST BLESSINGS AND RELICS FOR LYDIA

With her champion soul up for grabs in a Champion Training Tournament (or, more realistically, being such a good champion that she’s likely to stay on your wishlist until you eventually get a soul), picking the right Blessing and Relic for Lydia is an important decision for every account.

Best Blessings

There are several great options for Blessings for Lydia:

  1. Cruelty: this adds a ton of damage against all bosses and waves and it’s worth having the highest rank possible on each team.
  2. Brimstone: this is a great option against bosses and is an extra debuff for 9-pc Feral.
  3. Temporal Chains: her best overall arena option, and a decent pick against wave content where enemies get buffs.
  4. Nature’s Wrath: this is the best option if you’re building Lydia for all-out damage.

Best Relics

There are several great options for Relics for Lydia as well:

  1. Murderfang (Mythical Clan Relic): this is particuarly well suited to Lydia since it can trigger off all her counterattacks.
  2. Volcardiac Heart (Mythical Standard Relic): this is a great option for boosting your team’s survivability and fits well if you’re building RES too.
  3. Perched Shadow (Epic Faction Wars Relic): a great budget pick, this will help Lydia cycle through her abilities more often.
  4. Irethi Coronet (Epic Chimera Relic): this is a great option if you’re building Lydia for all-out damage.

BEST AREAS FOR LYDIA

Lydia is one of the rare champions who earns a prestigious 5-star rating from us! It’s hard to pick best areas for Lydia because she’s pretty much top tier everywhere.

Chimera

Lydia is top tier against every Clan Boss but stands out as particularly good against the Chimera due to the sheer number of Trials she enables and her ease of use as a void affinity champion. If you build her with the lowest C.DMG% on the team, she can even be your Ram Form target.

PvE > PvP

The one piece of advice we would give is to pick between PvP or PvE for building Lydia–and we would recommend PvE for 99% of players.

While Lydia can be viable in Arena, she is so ubiquitously useful against bosses that it’s not worth investing into Arena at the expense of those areas. You’re usually using her for her passive in Arena/Siege anyway, which will work just fine even if she’s not geared in Stoneskin or Reaction etc.

LET US KNOW WHAT YOU THINK OF LYDIA THE DEATHSIREN IN THE COMMENTS!

title: Lydia Perfect Soul Champion Training Event
excerpt_html: Let’s break down everything you need to know about this event, why it matters, and how you can get yourself a Perfect Lydia Soul.
content_html:
Lydia Perfect Soul Champion Training Event

Raid: Shadow Legends is bringing out a big one this Monday—a Champion Training Tournament with some seriously high-stakes rewards. And at the top of the prize pool? A Perfect Soul for Lydia, one of the game’s most powerful support champions. Whether you’re a veteran or midgame player, this event could be your best shot at grabbing an endgame-tier blessing—but it won’t come easy.

Let’s break down everything you need to know about this event, why it matters, and how you can give yourself the best chance of finishing at the top.

What’s this Event all about?

Starting today, Raid is launching a 96-hour Champion Training Tournament. The goal is simple: level up and develop your champions. That includes:

  • Levelling up your champs
  • Ranking them up
  • Ascending them
  • Using skill books

It’s one of the more accessible events in Raid, but this time, there’s a twist. The winner of each leaderboard (1 out of 100 players) will receive a Perfect Soul for Lydia, with the star rank based on your account level:

  • Level 30–39: 3★ Perfect Soul
  • Level 40–59: 4★
  • Level 60–99: 5★
  • Level 100: 6★

A 6-star soul is a huge deal. You’re not just getting a small stat bump—you’re gaining a massive boost to HP, attack, defense, resistance, accuracy, and even speed. Plus, depending on the blessing you pick, the added effects can completely change how a champion performs in content like Hydra, Doom Tower, or Arena.

Top Strategies to Maximize Points

If you’re aiming for first place, you need to come in with a plan. Here are the three main approaches players are taking:

1. Go Hard, Go Early

Jump out in front early on Monday. The idea here is to intimidate the competition with a high points lead. Burn your resources fast—brews, skill books, potions—and hope that others won’t bother trying to chase you.

2. Play the Long Game

Use all your energy in the campaign each day. Slow and steady means less upfront investment, and you can pace yourself over the four-day window. But beware: if someone surges on the last day, you may not be able to catch up.

3. Snipe at the End

This is the sneakiest (and often most effective) strategy. Stay within striking distance, then drop all your books and brews in the final few hours. Many players will go from 3rd to 1st place in 10 minutes with a stash of resources.

Be Careful: The hidden risks

This event might seem straightforward, but it’s incredibly resource-intensive. If you don’t plan properly, you could blow through books, energy, and brews—and still walk away empty-handed.

Important watchouts:

  • Know your limits: If you don’t have a deep stockpile, you may not be able to compete with top-tier players who have thousands of brews or hundreds of skill books ready.
  • Check your leaderboard: The luck of your grouping will play a big role. Some brackets are super competitive; others might be more manageable.
  • Avoid resource FOMO: Don’t dump everything unless you’re serious about winning. Many players get baited into wasting resources chasing top 3, only to be sniped at the end.

Final Thoughts

This is shaping up to be one of Raid’s most contested events in recent memory. A Perfect Soul for Lydia is a powerful prize, and 6-star blessings are game-changing. But with only one winner per bracket, it’s a tough race to run.

If you’ve got the resources and are ready to go all-in, this could be your moment. Otherwise, don’t feel bad about sitting it out or going for milestones instead of leaderboard placement. Sometimes, the smartest play is saving your stash for the next big event.

Let us know what approach you’re taking in the comments!

title: Top 10 Epic Poisoners for the Demon Lord Clan Boss!
excerpt_html: Today, we dive into the Top 10 Epic Poisoners for the Demon Lord Clan Boss, each offering added utility for your team in Raid: Shadow Legends.
content_html:
Top 10 Epic Poisoners for the Demon Lord Clan Boss!

The Poison debuff is one of the best ways to deal consistent damage to bosses like the Dragon – and in this instance, the Demon Lord Clan Boss. This effect deals damage at the start of the target’s turn – unless instantly activated by an ally – for either 2.5% or 5% of their MAX HP, depending on the version applied. Most champions from Rare rarity and above place the stronger 5% version.

In today’s list, we’re focusing mainly on Epic champions who not only apply Poisons but also bring additional utility to your team. Whether it’s through buffs, debuffs, or raw damage, these extra effects are key to building a well balanced Clan Boss team. The best poisoner for your team often comes down to the type of setup you’re running, which is why the champions in this list are not ranked in any particular order.

If you’re after a wider list of Poisoners across all rarities, be sure to check out the link below!

View All Poisoners Here

Fahrakin

Fahrakin has stood the test of time as a staple in many players’ Clan Boss teams. When it comes to Poisons, he places two alongside a HP Burn through his A2. This balance of debuffs is ideal, as it avoids overcrowding the debuff bar and leaves room for other key effects your team may bring. He also provides an Ally Attack to boost overall damage output and increase the frequency of A1 debuff placements. On top of that, he boosts allies’ Crit Rate and Crit Damage, places Decrease Defence on his A1, and deals solid raw damage with his A2. For survivability, 20% of the damage Fahrakin would take is instead spread evenly across all allies.

View Fahrakin The Fat Guide

Venomage

Heal Reduction doesn’t directly impact the Clan Boss, but Venomage uses it in a unique way – reducing the damage allies take by 15% when the debuff is active. This makes Venomage a great option for team sustain, and they also place two 5% Poisons alongside this effect. For added utility, the A2 applies Decrease Defence, and if the target is under Poison, it also applies Decrease Attack. The A1 has a chance to activate Poison debuffs, tying the kit together with strong synergy. Venomage helps reduce incoming damage, apply Poisons, and boost your team’s overall output. Plus, the 45 Accuracy Aura is a big help for progressing accounts.

View Venomage Guide

Fayne

Both Decrease Defence and Weaken are key debuffs for boosting your team’s damage, and Fayne brings both through her A3, while also healing herself based on the number of debuffs on the target. Her A2 adds even more value by placing Decrease Attack to reduce incoming damage, along with two 5% Poisons for consistent damage output. When combined, these effects make her an incredibly well rounded Epic – offering strong debuffs, and raw damage with the right build.

View Fayne Guide

Dark Kael

This champion is the fourth Epic you’ll acquire by collecting fragments from Secret Rooms in the Normal Doom Tower. What makes him stand out is his passive, which reduces the Critical Rate of enemies under two or more Poisons by 15% – a great way to prevent your team from being wiped by unlucky crits. He also brings strong Poison utility, applying three Poisons and Poison Sensitivity with his A3, while his A2 places Decrease Attack and extends all debuffs on the target. This combination ensures consistent damage, reduces incoming damage, and helps keep debuffs active for longer.

View Dark Kael Guide

Toragi The Frog

Unlike other champions on today’s list, Toragi applies Poisons through his passive when he’s hit. With a 50% chance to place a Poison per hit, his value really shines against affinity Clan Boss, where he’s recieving multiple hits. Combined with his Ally Protection, Shield, and self-healing, he’s a strong option for keeping your team & himself alive while contributing damage over time. On top of that, his A1 brings Decrease Attack, helping further reduce the damage your team takes.

View Toragi The Frog Guide

Urogrim

Urogrim is a simple yet highly effective support champion for Clan Boss. His A3 places two 5% Poisons on enemies while applying a Continuous Heal to all allies, offering both strong damage over time and HP sustain. He can also remove all debuffs from an ally and heal them by 40%, making him perfect for cleansing your lead Stun target and restoring their health. On top of that, his A1 has a chance to place additional Poisons, adding even more value to his kit.

View Urogrim Guide

Aox

Aox really shines when paired with champions that bring Ally Attack or Counterattack to the team. This is thanks to his A1, which has a 30% chance per hit to apply Poison – making those extra hits valuable. What earns him a spot on this list is the rest of his supportive kit: he brings Decrease Attack to reduce incoming damage, a team heal that scales based on the number of debuffs on the target, and a passive that can extend the duration of 2 random debuffs when he’s attacked, helping to keep your key effects active longer.

View Aox Guide

Occult Brawler

Occult Brawler is the only champion on this list who brings no additional utility outside of Poison, but his value lies in how effective he is without books. Most of his Poison output comes from his passive, which has a 70% chance to place a 4 turn Poison on a target at the start of each turn. His A1 also has a 50% chance to place a Poison without books, with the option to boost it to 60% if you choose to invest. For accounts that are still building out their roster, having such a reliable Poisoner that doesn’t require Skill Tomes is a huge win, and you can focus his build more toward survivability if you’re struggling to keep him alive.

View Occult Brawler Guide

Liburga

One downside to this champion is how long it takes to acquire her, as she’s the eighth champion you’ll earn fragments for through the Normal Doom Tower Secret Rooms. That said, she brings a strong Poison kit, placing two 5% Poisons and Poison Sensitivity with her A2, along with a chance to place more Poisons through her A1. Her passive also has a chance to increase the duration of Poison when she’s attacked, helping keep them active for longer. On top of that, Luburga provides both Increase Attack and Increase Defence, boosting your team’s damage while improving survivability.

View Liburga Guide

Anax

This champion has been a staple in both Non-Unkillable and Unkillable teams since his release – and for good reason. His A3 and A1 reliably apply Decrease Defence and Weaken to boost your team’s damage output, while also providing self-healing based on the damage he deals. He’s also capable of strong raw damage across all his skills when built correctly. His Poisons come from the A2, with each hit having a chance to place both Poison and Decrease Accuracy. Additionally, he can self buff with Increase Attack or Increase Defence when his HP drops below certain thresholds.

View Anax Guide

HONOURABLE MENTIONS

As mentioned at the start, this top 10 list mainly focused on champions who bring extra utility alongside their Poison debuffs – whether that’s buffs, debuffs, or just helping your team in more ways than one. That said, Poison is Poison, and depending on the type of team you’re running – Like a specific Unkillable comp where Poison is all you want to fill a gap – or if you simply don’t have access to the champions listed above, here are some other strong options worth considering:

SUIREN

  • Although this champion shines most when paired with Ally Attack or Counterattack champions, she earns an hounourable mention for similar reasons to Fayne. Her kit includes Decrease Defence and Weaken to boost your team’s damage, along with a 50% chance per hit on her A1 to place Poisons. While counterattacks or Ally Attacks help maximize her Poison application, even without them, she still brings a strong mix of utility to a Clan Boss team.

STEELSKULL

  • This champion also pairs well with Ally Attack or Counterattack setups, giving more opportunities to land Poisons through his A1, which has a 35% chance per hit to place one. On top of that, he provides Increase Defence and team-wide healing for sustain, along with a single target heal and cleanse – perfect for removing the Stun debuff from your stun target and restoring their health right after.

MYCILIAC PRIEST ORN

  • Myciliac is a true example of someone fully built around Poisons, with the ability to place or activate them across all of his skills. His A3 places two 5% Poisons and Poison Sensitivity, while also healing him for each Poison applied. This works perfectly with his passive, which increases his Defence by 5% – up to 25% – for every Poison activated on enemies. He can instantly trigger those Poisons with his A2, and his A1 brings even more chances to place them.

URTICATA

  • Urticata is unique in how she applies Poisons, as none of her active skills directly place them. Instead, her A3 applies Hex, and her passive grants a 25% chance to place a Poison whenever she or an ally lands a critical hit on the Hexed target. In theory, this turns your entire team into poisoners – just be mindful of the debuff bar filling up quickly. She also brings Poison Sensitivity and extends the duration of Poisons with her A1, making her a strong option for certain Unkillable team setups.

ROWAN

  • Rowan can stack a loads of Poisons for your team, thanks to her A3 hitting four times with a 100% chance to place a Poison on each hit. The main drawback is that she’s quite fragile, so surrounding her with the right support – or using her in an Unkillable team is the safest option. Outside of this skill, her kit doesn’t offer much for Clan Boss, with Heal Reduction and Stun effects that aren’t really needed. However, all those multi-hits make her a great candidate for triggering Giant Slayer procs.

CORPSE COLLECTOR

  • This champion has flown under the radar for years, but actually has a solid kit for Clan Boss. Her A3 delivers three random hits, each with a chance to place a Poison – perfect for Clan Boss, where all hits focus on a single target. She also applies Veil to herself, helping reduce incoming AOE damage for added sustain. On top of that, her A1 can place Leech, allowing your team to heal from the damage they deal.

TAYA

  • Taya is another you can acquire by collecting fragments from Secret Rooms in the Normal Doom Tower. While she can deal solid raw damage through her active skills, her standout feature is the ability to place three 5% Poisons with her triple hitting A2. To complement this, her A3 can instantly activate both HP Burn and Poison effects – especially useful if you’re running a burner in your team. Unfortunately, she also brings two less useful debuffs for Clan Boss – Heal Reduction and Decrease Accuracy – which can clutter the debuff bar.

JULIANA

  • Since her slight buff a couple of years ago, Juliana has become a solid option to consider once again. She was once one of the go to champions for Clan Boss in the early days of Raid, and we’d love to hear if any of you are still using her today! Her A2 applies Increase Crit Rate to herself, meaning you only need to build her with 70% Crit Rate, and if both hits are critical, she places two 5% Poisons with a 3-turn duration. Her A3 strips buffs from the target and places HP Burn, which is great for removing Increase Attack from the affinity Clan Boss. Just make sure she reaches 100% Crit Rate to avoid Turn Meter disruption from her A1, and be aware her main weakness is against Force affinity.

LET US KNOW IN THE COMMENTS WHICH EPIC CLAN BOSS POISONERS YOU LIKE THE MOST!

title: Lady Mikage Summon Events
excerpt_html: Plarium has announced a set of Summon Events that will feature Champions required for the Lady Mikage Fusion, Get your hands on your Mythical Champion!
content_html:
Lady Mikage Summon Events

Plarium has announced a set of Summon Events that will feature Champions required for the Lady Mikage Fusion, Get your hands on your Mythical Champion by picking up any Champions you are missing if you have the shards available.

Of course, these events are dropping in the middle of a Fusion Event; where we have a Hero’s Path Event upcoming on the weekend – so you may opt to save your shards for that instead, especially if you have already completed the Lady Mikage Fusion, this one is a hard-pass for you!

Let’s check out Plarium’s announcements…

View Lady Mikage Fusion

Plarium Announcement

Gauranteed Events:

Hey y’all!

I have some news about the upcoming events

Next week, we are planning to launch several Guaranteed events:

  • This Monday, April 14th, summon Champions using Ancient Shards while the event is active, and you are guaranteed to get Locwain by your 40th Ancient Shard.
  • This Tuesday, April 15th, summon Champions using Ancient Shards while the event is active, and you are guaranteed to get Ilysinya by your 40th Ancient Shard.

These events will last 24 hours.

  • This Wednesday, April 16th, summon Champions using Ancient Shards while the event is active, and you are guaranteed to get Endalia by your 40th Ancient Shard.

This event will last 23 hours. Remember, if you do happen to summon the Champion before your 40th Ancient Shard, your event will end.

Progressive Summon Events:

Also, this Tuesday, April 15th, we’re planning to launch Progressive Chance Event, giving you at least x15 chance to summon the Champions of your choice from the selected pool from Ancient, Primal, and Sacred Shards.

1 Slot Legendary Champions:

  • Tatsu 

1 Slot Epic Champions:

  • Aeshma 
  • Eolfrig
  • Delaaja

This event will last 24 hours.

And this Wednesday, April 16th, we’re planning to launch Progressive Chance Event, giving you at least x15 chance to summon the Champions of your choice from the selected pool from Ancient, Primal, and Sacred Shards.

1 Slot Legendary Champions:

  • Keeyra the Watcher

1 Slot Epic Champions:

  • Lady Quilen 
  • Enda Moonbeam 
  • Fyr-gun Isbeil

This event will last 23 hours.

Our Thoughts on this event

Overall; this is a fantastic opportunity to pick up the final components you may be missing for your Mikage Fusion, but it is important to consider we are in the middle of the current Fusion Event, where we have no only had a Summon Rush Event this weekend, but at the end of this week we will get a Hero’s Path Event which will require Summoning, in the place of the Champion Chase Tournament we are used to having with Fusions.

However, Lady Mikage is a VERY impactful Champion for every account, so if you have the shards available and have not yet completed the Fusion, this remains a great opportunity to finish it up and get your hands on a very powerful Mythical Champion who can change your account for the better!

Are you going for any of these Lady Mikage Summon Events? Let us know!

title: Top 10 Rare Champions for the Demon Lord Clan Boss!
excerpt_html: YST & Deadwood Jedi break down their Top 10 Rare Champions for the Demon Lord Clan Boss in Raid: Shadow Legends.
content_html:
Top 10 Rare Champions for the Demon Lord Clan Boss!

As part of the Free to Play Challenge 2025, Deadwood Jedi and YST teamed up to share their personal top 10 rare champions for tackling the Demon Lord Clan Boss. While most rares won’t carry a team into Ultra Nightmare, they can still play a valuable role in filling gaps within a squad. However, there are some exceptions – champions such as Heiress and Painkeeper are great in Unkillable teams, and some rares can push you into higher difficulties & perform surprisingly well in the right setup.

With the addition of blessings in Raid: Shadow Legends, some, like Nature’s Bounty, can significantly enhance a champion’s effectiveness by upgrading weaker debuffs into stronger versions. This means certain rares may be more valuable than ever, especially with a high star soul. In this article, YST and Deadwood’s selections are based on champions they believe could make a real impact during the free to play series, including those they would consider maxing within the three months to take their team to the next level.

View Our Clan Boss Guide

Coffin Smasher

YST made a big mistake early in the series by feeding this champion in his rush to get his first six star. Since then, he hasn’t pulled another champion with a solid Decrease Attack and regrets the decision! Having a rare champion who can apply the stronger version of Decrease Attack with a triple hitting skill at a 30% chance is valuable, especially when combined with HP Burn as a damage source. On top of that, this champion reduces incoming damage from AOE attacks by 5%, the same value provided by the Blastproof mastery.

View Coffin Smasher Guide

Apothecary

Deadwood chose Apothecary as his first pick, an epic level champion disguised as a rare! His three turn cooldown Increase Speed and Turn Meter fill allow allies to cycle turns faster, whether in general progression or fine tuned for a 2:1 ratio in Clan Boss. He also brings a strong single target heal and a triple hitting A1, making him great for Giant Slayer procs. Even if he gets replaced in your Clan Boss team later on, he remains a valuable champion worth maxing for other areas of the game.

View Apothecary Guide

Denid The Tusk Knight

Next on the list is the Tusk Knight, a rare with an ability that even among epics is extremely rare. He can place a 25% Strengthen buff on all allies on a 3 turn cooldown, the same skill Morag provides with her A2. Strengthen is one of the strongest buffs in the game, reducing incoming damage by 15% or 25%, depending on the version applied. This alone makes Denid a standout rare, but he also brings utility with an A1 Provoke, which can be useful in encounters like Magma Dragon or the Head of Decay during progression.

View Denid The Tusk Knight Guide

Bulwark

No top rares list would be complete without the Clan Boss legend, Bulwark. He starts with a 17% Defence Aura in all battles, making him a solid choice for the leader position in Clan Boss. His A2 applies HP Burn for damage ticks, while his A1 places a 15% Weaken debuff to boost your team’s damage output. If you’re lucky enough to pull a high star soul, Nature’s Bounty could upgrade his Weaken to the stronger version. His passive also gives him a chance to extend the duration of debuffs on attackers when hit, helping to keep debuffs active for longer.

View Bulwark Guide

Petrifya Rockroot

Frozen Banshee is always a top choice for a dedicated Poisoner, though both Deadwood and YST started with Kael to fill that role in this series. However, Deadwood has his eye on Petrifya Rockroot, who could reach another level with Nature’s Bounty. This blessing could upgrade her AOE 25% Decrease Attack to the stronger version, and she also applies Poison Sensitivity, Poisons & Decrease Defence, making her a well rounded option for debuffing. Even if she isn’t the primary Decrease Attack champion, she serves as a strong backup, ensuring coverage when the debuff drops off, such as if Coffin Smasher fails to place his.

View Petrifya Rockroot Guide

Marked

If you’re looking for a champion who provides both Increase Defence and Block Debuffs, Marked is a solid choice. With rare skill tomes, she can keep these buffs on a three turn cooldown, helping sustain your team while blocking effects like Decrease Speed, or buff extended to cover the Stun phase.
